channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Allstate sued for allegedly tracking and selling 45M Americans' location data
item.description: Many apps unrelated to location still ask users for tracking permission, and Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton has filed a lawsuit seeking to end this alleged practice by Allstate.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Samsung Galaxy Z Flip 7 leak gives us another hint about one of its key specs
item.description: Matching up with previous rumors, a new leak suggests the Exynos 2500 will power the Galaxy Z Flip 7.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Meta wants everyone to know that it, too, is investing a lot in AI
item.description: Not to be outdone by its close rival OpenAI, Meta has announced its plans to spend $60 to $65 billion on AI infrastructure this year, and is building a data center almost as big as Manhattan.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Smile! You can now control your Chromebook with your face
item.description: Google has started rolling out a number of new features in Chrome OS, according to The Verge, including a new feature that allows a user to control their Chromebook using head movements (to move the mouse pointer) and facial expressions (to click or activate dictation).
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: California lab makes new discovery in search for HIV vaccine
item.description: A unique reaction in which antibodies bind to other antibodies may help scientists at Scripps Research better understand how to design a vaccine
item.dc:creator: Ashley Mackin Solomon, The Mercury News

item.title: Sony WH-1000XM6: 2025 Flagship Headphones Get Bigger Battery, Improved Hinge
item.description: Details of the Sony WH-1000XM6 headphones have been filed with the US FCC, revealing improved battery life and a redesigned hinge. The new model will feature Bluetooth 5.3 and a possible low-energy connection for control.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Trump AI project presents Musk with early test of loyalty
item.description: Elon Musk’s criticism of an artificial intelligence (AI) infrastructure project backed by President Trump is presenting an early test of how the SpaceX CEO will balance his personal conflicts in the technology space with the interests of the president.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: UK CMA opens investigations into Apple and Google mobile ecosystems
item.description: Apple and Google could end up having to make apps and app content “easier” to obtain from elsewhere, and offer fairer terms to app developers hosting their content on its official stores.
item.dc:creator: luke.hughes@futurenet.com (Luke Hughes), TechRadar

item.title: First image for Supergirl: Woman of Tomorrow shows Milly Alcock's Kara Zor-El in a place that'll be very familiar to DC comic book fans
item.description: James Gunn has revealed the first image for Supergirl: Woman of Tomorrow as filming gets underway on the DCU Chapter One movie.
item.dc:creator: tom.power@futurenet.com (Tom Power), TechRadar

item.title: Los líderes empresariales prevén centrar la inversión en innovación, eficiencia y resiliencia
item.description: En un contexto de continuada incertidumbre en el mercado global, los líderes empresariales parecen haber arrancado el año con una dosis extra de positividad respecto a las perspectivas de su organización.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: ADL, which had defended Musk, takes issue with his Nazi puns
item.description: The Anti-Defamation League (ADL), a nonprofit committed to fighting antisemitism, criticized tech billionaire Elon Musk for his social media post Thursday that used Nazi puns to taunt those who accused him of making a fascist salute at an event following President Trump’s inauguration on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Linkedin sued for training AI on users’ private messages
item.description: A lawsuit in California accuses Linkedin of using private messages on its platform to train AI models, according to the BBC.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Spain's leader wants the EU to ‘make social media great again.’ Here's how
item.description: Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez is launching a crusade against what he sees as the downsides of social media.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Wilson And Jamey Keaten, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Revolutionizing procurement: How AI drives efficiency & profitability
item.description: AI is set to transform business operations, with the complex area of procurement ripe for digital innovation that will drive tangible impact. Procurement plays a crucial role in organizational success because it directly impacts operational efficiency, cost-effectiveness, and customer satisfaction.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Tips on overcoming the loss of cherished, personal belongings in disasters
item.description: Losing sentimental belongings can be traumatic for those who go through disasters that destroy homes.
item.dc:creator: Julia Rubin, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: How to cope when disasters strip away photos, heirlooms and other pieces of the past
item.description: They’re the possessions that tell your story: the photos of old friends and relatives. The ring your mom left you. The hand-knit Christmas stockings. Your grandfather's secretary desk and the letters inside.
item.dc:creator: JULIA RUBIN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Pakistan's parliament passes bill with sweeping controls on social media
item.description: ISLAMABAD (AP) — Pakistan’s lower house of parliament on Thursday passed a controversial bill that will give the government sweeping controls on social media, including sending users to prison for spreading disinformation.
item.dc:creator: MUNIR AHMED, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Screen breaks and the right desk setup offer relief from work-related eye strain
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The trouble started every day at around 3 p.m., after Cathy Higgins had spent five or six hours staring at an array of computer screens at her desk. Her university job overseeing research projects involved peering closely at numbers and details on contracts, applications and budgets.
item.dc:creator: CATHY BUSSEWITZ, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Emergency Response Transformed: Inside Kazakhstan’s SOS 102 System
item.description: Emergency response systems worldwide are undergoing rapid transformation, and Kazakhstan is no exception. With the introduction of the SOS 102 app, the country has set a new standard for public safety, redefining how law enforcement interacts with citizens.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Cisco CEO Robbins on AI: Pressure to deploy is real
item.description: Interest in AI has exploded over the last two or three years, but enterprises are only just beginning to think about how they’re going to take advantage of it, Cisco CEO Chuck Robbins told the audience at the company’s recent AI Summit.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Amazon to close 7 warehouses in the Canadian province of Quebec and eliminate 1,700 jobs
item.description: Online retailer Amazon said Wednesday that it's closing all seven of its warehouses in the Canadian province of Quebec in the next two months. The e-commerce giant said the move would provide “even more savings to our customers over the long run.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: MrBeast not quite in the bidding race for TikTok just yet, rep says
item.description: Jimmy Donaldson — better known online as MrBeast — isn't in the TikTok bidding race just yet, according to a representative for the YouTube star. Donaldson stirred interest in a Jan. 13 post on X saying he’d “buy TikTok so it doesn’t get banned.
item.dc:creator: JAMES POLLARD, HALELUYA HADERO and WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump administration shuts down White House Spanish-language page and social media
item.description: Within hours of President Donald Trump’s inauguration, the new administration took down the Spanish-language version of the official White House website. The site — currently https://www.whitehouse.gov/es/ — now gives users an “Error 404” message.
item.dc:creator: FERNANDA FIGUEROA, Associated Press, KRON4
